Home / Glossary / Descriptive Statistics
Glossary

What is descriptive statistics?

Updated 2026-04-15

Descriptive statistics are summary numbers — mean, median, mode, standard deviation, min, max, and percentiles — that describe the shape of a dataset without making predictions. They are the first thing every analyst computes after loading a file because they reveal scale, skew, and obviously broken columns in seconds.

5-number summary
min, Q1, median, Q3, max — Tukey's compact descriptor that fits on a box plot and explains a column at a glance.
Tukey, Exploratory Data Analysis (1977)
Mean ≠ Median
When the absolute gap exceeds 10–15% of the standard deviation, the column is meaningfully skewed and the mean alone will mislead.
Wilcox, Modern Robust Methods

See also: how to compare this quarter vs last quarter · how to A/B test a pricing change · DataVision Pro vs Excel.

The core measures

  • Central tendency — mean, median, mode.
  • Spread — variance, standard deviation, IQR, range.
  • Shape — skewness, kurtosis.
  • Position — percentiles, quartiles.

When the mean lies

Income, revenue, page-view, and time-on-site distributions are almost always right-skewed. Reporting only the mean overstates the typical experience. Always pair mean with median, or report the full 5-number summary. DataVision Pro's descriptive-statistics table includes both and flags skew automatically.

Frequently asked questions

Is descriptive statistics the same as inferential statistics?

No. Descriptive describes the data you have. Inferential generalises from a sample to a larger population using probability.

Should I report mean or median?

Report both. Mean is sensitive to outliers; median is robust. The gap between them is itself a useful diagnostic.

What's a good standard deviation?

There is no universal answer — std is meaningful only relative to the mean (coefficient of variation = std / mean) or to a domain benchmark.

Why does DataVision Pro show currency codes in the stats table?

Because mean revenue of 1,200 means very different things in JPY vs USD — the unit is part of the answer, not metadata.

Are percentiles better than std for skewed data?

Usually yes. P50/P90/P99 describe customer experience more honestly than mean ± std when the distribution has a long tail.

Related terms

Data Cleaning

The process of detecting and correcting errors, inconsistencies, missing values, and duplicates in a dataset before analysis.

Outlier Detection

Statistical techniques for finding observations that lie an abnormal distance from the rest of a dataset.

Predictive Analytics

Using historical data and statistical or ML models to forecast future outcomes or behaviours.

Data Drift

A change over time in the statistical properties of input data that degrades a model's accuracy.
